mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate reduces the equity discount rate and financing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as discounted cash flows and risk-taking increase.
failure_condition: Reject if over any 2-year rolling window the Rank IC of FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns is < 0.03 or periods of FEDFUNDS decline do not produce higher median 20-day returns than non-decline periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, tighter monetary conditions raise discount rates and short-term funding costs, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk premia widen and liquidity tightens.
failure_condition: Reject if in instances with FEDFUNDS > 2.5% fewer than 60% of 5-day forward returns are negative over an out-of-sample 3-year test or the negative association is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous CPIAUCSL month-on-month increase greater than 0.5% together with a FEDFUNDS rise > 0.25 percentage points within one month signals accelerating inflation with active policy response; the interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% MoM AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp within 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if when both trigger conditions occur fewer than 50% of 20-day forward returns are negative across a 3-year test period, or the interaction term's IC is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicate stronger consumer demand and near-term revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations and cyclical sentiment improve.
failure_condition: Reject if increases in RSAFS are not associated with higher median 5-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% over the prior three months signals a contraction in residential investment and demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations and related sector earnings deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by >5% over prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if in triggered episodes more than 50% of 60-day forward returns are non-negative over a 5-year evaluation or the negative effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated (>1.5%) and industrial production (INDPRO) falls by more than 0.5% month-on-month, the joint supply slowdown under tighter policy reduces expected profits and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if when the trigger condition holds fewer than 55% of 60-day forward returns are negative over a 3-year out-of-sample window or the interaction effect is not distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A growing payrolls series (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and higher aggregate income, supporting corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days via improved earnings expectations and risk appetite.
failure_condition: Reject if higher PAYEMS growth deciles do not show systematically higher median 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.2 percentage points in one month together with a fall in payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 50,000 in the same month signals rapid labor-market deterioration and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month AND PAYEMS falls > 50,000 in 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not produce negative 5-day returns in at least 60% of cases over a 3-year evaluation, or the joint signal's IC is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
